results.title: Morgan Stanley upgrades these two stocks on AI-led ABF substrate up-cycle By Investing.com
excerpts:
Section Title: Morgan Stanley upgrades these two stocks on AI-led ABF substrate up-cycle
Content:
The firm now expects earnings to expand sharply between 2025 and 2028, projecting a 105% compound annual growth rate for Unimicron and 113% for Nan Ya PCB. It raised its price target on Unimicron to NT$500 from NT$120.75 and on Nan Ya PCB to NT$515 from NT$165, implying 36% and 24% upside, respectively.
Morgan Stanley said this cycle differs from past PC-driven swings, which were more volatile and consumer-led. It argued that AI-related demand provides longer visibility and could support multiple expansion, with current valuations reflecting near-term price increases but not fully pricing in AI-driven volume growth and tighter supply from 2027.
The brokerage maintained an Overweight rating on Samsung Electro-Mechanics but kept an Underweight on Ibiden, citing more stretched expectations after a sharp rally.
Morgan Stanley upgrades these two stocks on AI-led ABF substrate up-cycle

results.title: ABF substrates: A Quiet Opportunity in the Semiconductor Industry - Thornburg Investment Management®
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Chipmakers are increasing investments in select ABF suppliers
Content:
Accordingly, a number of chipmaking giants — among them Intel, AMD and Nvidia — have subsidized roughly 50% of the capital-expansion projects of four key high-end ABF players (Ibiden, Shinko, Unimicron and AT&S) due to their ability to handle the technical complexity required for successful and precise advanced substrate production processes.
In addition, these highly sought-after ABF makers are becoming entrenched not only in their customers’ manufacturing process, but also in research and development. This makes ABF materials even less fungible across suppliers. Chipmakers’ level of capital investment and partnership has resulted in high barriers to entry for the advanced substrates market, further distinguishing higher-quality ABF manufacturers from the rest of the pack.
 ... 
Section Title: ABF substrates: A Quiet Opportunity in the Semiconductor Industry > ... > Stay Connected
Content:
Subscribe now to stay up-to-date with Thornburg’s news and insights.

Section Title: About IBIDEN News

2026/02/03
About IBIDEN
Press Release
Section Title: Notice Regarding Capital Investment Plan for High-Performance IC Package Substrates
Content:
IBIDEN CO., LTD. (Head Office: Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture, Representative Director and President: Koji Kawashima) (hereinafter "the Company") hereby announces that the Board of Directors, at a meeting held today, resolved to approve a capital investment plan for its electronics business, amounting to approximately 500 billion yen over the three-year period from fiscal year 2026 to fiscal year 2028, with the aim of achieving the fiscal year 2030 targets disclosed in May last year. As the first phase of this plan, the Company hereby announces the resolution to implement additional capital investment, primarily for the existing Gama Plant (Cell6), for the purpose of expanding the production capacity of high-performance IC package substrates, mainly for high-performance servers.
【Overview of the Capital Investment Plan】
 ... 
Section Title: Notice Regarding Capital Investment Plan for High-Performance IC Package Substrates
Content:
Details of the Capital Investment Plan |＜Place＞
Gama Plant and other existing plants, including those overseas
＜Investment Amount＞
Approximately 220 billion yen (planned)
＜Operation Start＞
Sequential operation and planned commencement of mass production from fiscal year 2027
＜Production Capacity＞
Through this investment, the Company will increase production capacity to meet the demand for high-performance IC package substrates from fiscal year 2027 onwards |
Section Title: Notice Regarding Capital Investment Plan for High-Performance IC Package Substrates
Content:
The Gama Plant (Cell6) was completed in fiscal year 2023, and while mass production at the Ono Plant has been prioritized, we have now aligned on the general direction with our customers and will proceed with further capital investment toward mass production. In addition, to respond to strong customer demand, we are currently considering various measures, such as further expansion of the Ono Plant's capacity. We will make timely and appropriate disclosures as soon as internal decisions are finalized.
Gama Plant (Cell6)

results.title: Top Chip Testing Stocks to Watch, According to Bernstein By Investing.com
excerpts:
Section Title: Top Chip Testing Stocks to Watch, According to Bernstein
Content:
BE Semiconductor recently reported a 43% sequential increase in preliminary fourth-quarter orders, driven by demand for data center applications. Separately, Bernstein SocGen Group lowered its price target on the company, citing a slower adoption of certain bonding technology.
**4. Ibiden -** The company has benefited significantly from ABF substrate upgrades and adoption of new technologies such as Embedded Multi-die Interconnect Bridge. Nvidia’s Rubin substrate migration is approaching, with ABF content value expected to double.
Ibiden is likely to regain share from Unimicron in Rubin products and maintain a dominant market share with Nvidia substrates.
*This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.*

Section Title: ... > DACH Investor Relevance in AI Supply Chain
Content:
German, Austrian, and Swiss investors increasingly allocate to Asian semis for AI themes, with Ibiden fitting as a mid-cap pick. Frankfurt-listed ETFs holding Tokyo stocks provide easy access without direct ADR hurdles. Handelsblatt noted on March 21, 2026, rising DACH interest in Japan tech amid U.S. valuation concerns.
Ibiden's ESG profile, with low carbon footprint in substrate production, aligns with European sustainability mandates. Dividend repatriation via Tokyo in JPY benefits from current FX levels. Compared to ASML or Infineon, Ibiden offers higher growth at reasonable multiples.

excerpts:
Section Title: Unimicron Technology Corp. > Overview > Description
Content:
Unimicron Technology Corp. engages in the manufacture and sale of printed circuit boards (PCB). Its products include rigid PCB, flexible printed circuit, integrated circuit (IC), flip chip substrate, memory module, SiP & RF module, wire bond substrate, package on package substrate, hybrid substrate, and IC test and burn-in service. The company was founded on January 25, 1990 and is headquartered in Taoyuan, Taiwan.

results.title: Notice Regarding Capital Investment for High-Performance ...
excerpts:
Note : This document has been translated from a part of the Japanese original for reference purposes only.
In the event of any discrepancy between this translated document and the Japanese original, the original shall prevail.
February 3, 2026
Company name: IBIDEN CO., LTD.
Representative: Koji Kawashima, President & CEO, Representative Director
Stock exchange listings: Tokyo and Nagoya Stock Exchange (Code number: 4062)
Contact: Katsutoshi Nishimura, Finance Division Manager, Strategic Corporate Planning Operation
Telephone: +81-584-81-3111
Notice Regarding Capital Investment for High-Performance IC Package Substrates
(Acquisition of Fixed Assets)
IBIDEN CO., LTD. (hereinafter “the Company”) hereby announces that the Board of Directors,
at a meeting held today, resolved to approve a capital investment plan for its electronics business,
amounting to approximately 500 billion yen over the three-year period from fiscal year 2026 to
fiscal year 2028, with the aim of achieving the fiscal year 2030 targets disclosed in May last year.
As the first phase of this plan, the Company hereby announces our resolution to implement
additional capital investment, primarily for the existing Gama Plant (Cell6), for the purpose of
expanding the production capacity of high-performance IC package substrates, mainly for high-
performance servers.
1. Overview of the Capital Investment Plan
1.
Purpose
: Expansion of Production Capacity for High-Performance
IC Package Substrates (for AI Servers and High-performance
Servers)
2.
Planned Period
: Fiscal Year 2026 to Fiscal Year 2028 (three-year period)
3.
Total Investment : Approximately 500 billion yen
4.
Target
: 1. Gama Plant (3-200 Gama-cho, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture)
and other existing plants, including those overseas
2. Ono Plant (237-1 Shimoiso, Ono-cho, Ibi-gun, Gifu Prefecture)
and other existing plants, including those overseas
5.
Details of the Capital Investment Plan:
<Place>
Gama Plant and other existing plants, including those
overseas
<Investment Amount>
Approximately 220 billion yen (planned)
<Operation Start>
Sequential operation and planned commencement of
mass production from fiscal year 2027
<Production Capacity>
Through this investment, the Company will increase
production capacity to meet the demand for high-
performance IC package substrates from fiscal year 2027
onwards
2. Future Outlook
The impact of this investment on the Company's consolidated financial results for the current
fiscal year is expected to be immaterial.
End

results.title: Top ABF substrate manufacturers and market leaders
excerpts:
Section Title: Top ABF substrate manufacturers and market leaders
Content:
[Home](https://www.wonderfulpcb.com/)
[Blog](https://www.wonderfulpcb.com/category/blog/)
Top ABF substrate manufacturers and market leaders
When you look at ABF substrate manufacturers in semiconductors, you see names like Ibiden, Unimicron, Shinko Electric, and Kinsus Interconnect. These companies are leaders because they make reliable products. They spend money on new technology. They also have big factories. The global ABF market reached almost $5 billion in 2024. It keeps growing quickly. Customers trust these top companies. They give a steady supply. They follow tough environmental rules. They work to make every chip perform better.
 ... 
Section Title: Top ABF substrate manufacturers and market leaders > ... > Unimicron Technology
Content:
Unimicron Technology is a top [abf substrate maker](https://www.wonderfulpcb.com/id/) . The company is from Taiwan. Its main office is in Taoyuan. Unimicron helps make Ajinomoto build-up film substrates. These materials connect advanced chips to circuit boards. Big companies like NVIDIA and Apple use Unimicron’s products. These products are important for CPUs and graphics chips. They are used in servers and gaming consoles.
Unimicron is strong in many ways:
It is a leader in the PCB industry.
The company makes high-density interconnect PCBs and flexible printed circuit boards.
Unimicron creates, builds, and sells many kinds of PCBs.
Its products are found in computers, phones, and other electronics.
Unimicron keeps growing by investing in new technology. You can count on Unimicron for reliable abf products in the fast-changing semiconductor world.
Section Title: Top ABF substrate manufacturers and market leaders > ... > Ibiden Co., Ltd.
Content:
Ibiden Co., Ltd. is another top abf substrate maker. The company started in 1912 as Ibigawa Electric Power Co., Ltd. At first, it made hydroelectric power. Later, Ibiden moved into electronics and ceramics. Now, it is known for high-quality PCBs and IC package substrates in the abf market.
Section Title: Top ABF substrate manufacturers and market leaders > ... > Ibiden Co., Ltd.
Content:
| Year | Event Description |
| 1912 | Started as Ibigawa Electric Power Co., Ltd. and made hydroelectric power. |
| 19XX | Began making electronics and ceramics. |
| Present | Known for high-quality PCBs and IC package substrates, especially ABF substrates. |
Section Title: Top ABF substrate manufacturers and market leaders > ... > Ibiden Co., Ltd.
Content:
Ibiden is strong in the ic substrate market. Here is a look at its market share and strengths:
 ... 
Section Title: Top ABF substrate manufacturers and market leaders > Market comparison > Production scale
Content:
| Manufacturer | Market Share (%) | ABF Substrate Share (%) |
| Unimicron | 17.7 | 26.6 |
| Nan Ya Printed Circuit Board | 10.3 | 13.5 |
| Ibiden | 9.7 | 14.6 |
| Samsung Electro-Mechanics | 9.1 | N/A |
| Shinko Electric Industries | 8.5 | 12.8 |
| AT&S | 6.1 | 8.0 |
Section Title: Top ABF substrate manufacturers and market leaders > Market comparison > Global reach
Content:
Some abf makers send their products all over the world. Unimicron has many types of abf substrates and works with big brands. Ibiden is known for making good and reliable products. Shinko Electric makes thin and high-density abf substrates for special jobs. Nan Ya gives good prices and has strong supply chains. If you care about the earth, Ibiden’s green work makes it a good choice.
Ibiden made abf substrates for 5G smartphones, showing it can handle big orders. Unimicron worked with top foundries on advanced packages, showing its design skills. Shinko Electric made high-frequency abf substrates for AI, showing strong performance.
Ibiden and Unimicron are good for big orders and trust. Ibiden and Shinko Electric are best for new ideas and flexible designs. Nan Ya is a smart pick for good prices. Ibiden is the best for eco-friendly abf products.
Section Title: Top ABF substrate manufacturers and market leaders > ABF substrates trends > Emerging players
Content:
The top five abf makers control about 70% of the market. Smaller companies own the other 30%. These smaller companies want to grow. They try new ideas to get more customers. Some work on making abf substrates that perform better. Others try to make their products cheaper or more reliable.
Many small companies use advanced technology to compete.
Some spend money on new materials for abf substrates.
A few use eco-friendly ways to make their products.
These companies want to keep up with the fast-changing semiconductor world.
Big companies like Ibiden, Shinko Electric Industries, and Unimicron still lead. They are strong in new ideas and making lots of products. Smaller companies need to find their own way. They often focus on special markets or offer unique packaging substrate solutions.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Which company leads the ABF substrate market?
Content:
Unimicron has the biggest share of the market. Ibiden, Nan Ya PCB, and Shinko Electric are also top companies. They spend money on new technology. They give products to big brands.

results.title: Nvidia Supplier Ibiden Weighs Faster Expansion for AI Demand
excerpts:
Section Title: Nvidia Supplier Ibiden Weighs Faster Expansion for AI Demand > In This Article:
Content:
[StockStory Top Pick](https://stockstory.org/high-quality/top-6-to-buy-this-week?partner=yahoo&utm_source=yahoo&utm_medium=content_ticker&utm_campaign=contenthighqualitystock)
NVDA -0.97%
IBIDY 0.00%
INTC +3.54%
(Bloomberg) -- Ibiden Co., the dominant supplier of chip package substrates used in Nvidia Corp.’s cutting-edge semiconductors, may need to dial up the pace of production capacity increases to keep up with demand, according to its chief executive officer.
Most Read from Bloomberg
 ... 
Section Title: Nvidia Supplier Ibiden Weighs Faster Expansion for AI Demand > In This Article:
Content:
Ibiden’s clients include Intel Corp., Advanced Micro Devices Inc., Samsung Electronics Co. and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., as well as Nvidia, according to data compiled by Bloomberg. Many of them consult with the Japanese company early in product development, because the substrates — which help transmit signals from semiconductors to the circuit board — need to be tailored for each chip. Substrates must be made to withstand the heat of an Nvidia graphics processing unit to form an AI chip package complete with components such as memory.
Section Title: Nvidia Supplier Ibiden Weighs Faster Expansion for AI Demand > In This Article:
Content:
Founded as a power utility company in 1912, Ibiden developed semiconductor expertise through a partnership with Intel that Kawashima cultivated by waiting every day in front of the Santa Clara company to stop engineers and executives for product feedback in the early 1990s. At one point, Intel comprised around 70% to 80% of Ibiden’s revenue from chip package substrates. That fell to around 30% in the fiscal year ended March as the US chipmaker struggled to execute a turnaround that recently saw the ousting of CEO Pat Gelsinger.
Reliance on Intel has hurt Ibiden’s stock, down around 40% this year. In October, Ibiden revised down its profit outlook after sluggish demand for components used in general purpose servers outweighed AI server-related growth. But while noting it was important to expand business with chipmakers other than Intel, Kawashima said he was confident Intel will bounce back.
Story Continues
 ... 
Section Title: Nvidia Supplier Ibiden Weighs Faster Expansion for AI Demand > In This Article:
Content:
All of Nvidia’s AI semiconductors now use Ibiden’s substrates, although Taiwanese rivals such as Unimicron Technology Corp. are eyeing the field. But it won’t be easy to break Ibiden’s position as dominant supplier, according to Toyo Securities analyst Hideki Yasuda.
“Nvidia’s AI chips need sophisticated substrates, and Ibiden is the only one that can mass produce them at a good production yield,” he said. “Taiwanese competitors won’t be able to take Ibiden’s share away by much.”
AI semiconductors earn more than 15% of Ibiden’s sales of around ¥370 billion ($2.3 billion), with that percentage expected to rise further. Nvidia’s said it’s begun full production of its next-generation Blackwell chips after encountering some initial technical challenges.
Read: Nvidia Partner Hon Hai Boosts Capacity to Meet ‘Crazy’ AI Demand
